_doc: VCBench reference values (VCBench (2026), §I.4 capability matrix + §I.3 reference test cases). Any drift in these values triggers test failure in tests/unit/test_reference_values.py. Edit only when the manuscript itself changes.

full_107._role: CANONICAL — used for VC Level decisions; real-control anchor is the principled cross-model convention (§I.3)

full_107_pred_anchor._role: FOR cell-eval cross-validation only — reproduces upstream cell-eval pearson_delta to 2e-6 absolute. NOT a competing canonical number. NOT used for VC Level decisions.

arc_state._convention_notes: Real-anchor is canonical because cross-model benchmarking requires a shared baseline — letting each model anchor on its own predicted control turns the metric into a per-model quantity, defeating the spec's shared-baseline premise. Pred-anchor exists solely so VCBench can numerically reconcile against upstream cell-eval reports.
arc_state._provenance: Norman 2019 K562, disjoint GEARS simulation split (seed=1, 139 train / 107 held-out test). Cross-validated under matched anchor conventions: vcbench.evaluate_dim_a(control_anchor='pred') = 0.4076 vs cell-eval pearson_delta = 0.4076 (agree to 2e-6, numerical precision). Under the canonical real-control anchor PRR = 0.4021.

pca_knn._source: results/dim_b/baselines_pca_knn_common.json::aggregate_native_macroF1 = 0.16647; native-protocol 5-tissue mean. Reported in the capability matrix as the PCA+kNN native baseline.

table2_kendall_tau_b._note_transcriptformer: 0.046 = unweighted mean of sci-fate (0.0510) + Weinreb (0.0414), matching the Dim E aggregation rule used for the other four methods. 0.041 is the Weinreb-only bootstrap value; the canonical aggregate is 0.046.
